justification: Both domain rules present with testable_assertions. All three architecture decisions present with implementation_notes (exceeds requirement). acceptance_criteria preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim. task_context is specific and actionable. No invented information. However, terminology_alignment does NOT address the Bird/MJ language difference specified in the rubric: Bird says 'SHIPPED' (order status language) and MJ says 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state' (technical endpoint language) — Magic should have explicitly reconciled that Bird's 'order status' and MJ's 'state' refer to the same concept. Instead, the terminology_alignment section maps generic domain-vs-technical terms without addressing the specific Bird/MJ divergence.

observations.text: Both invariant rules (state transition prohibition and refund precondition) captured with precise testable_assertions
Architecture guidance covers all three decisions (OrderStateMachine, PATCH endpoint, RefundService) with detailed implementation_notes
acceptance_criteria preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID error code verbatim and uses Given/When/Then format faithfully
task_context is a single paragraph that clearly tells Shaq what to build and why
No invented information — all details traceable to source summaries
open_questions correctly flags gaps like the incomplete transition matrix

terminology_alignment fails to address the specific Bird/MJ language divergence: Bird uses 'SHIPPED' and 'order status' while MJ uses 'state' in 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state'. The rubric explicitly requires Magic to confirm these refer to the same concept and clarify 'state' is the technical term for Bird's 'order status'.
terminology_alignment entries are generic domain-to-technical mappings rather than reconciling actual differences between the two source agents' language

agent_output_excerpt: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to turn these specs into production-ready code with tests.","domain_rules":[{"rule":
